name: pr-cycle
description: PreToolUse(Bash) PR full-cycle router for `gh pr create`, implemented in hexa-lang (`_pr_cycle.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Whitespace-tokenizes the command and scans for the `gh pr create` 3-gram anywhere — catches bare `gh pr create …` and chained forms like `git push && gh pr create …`. When matched (and not draft / not already merging), rewrites via the hook `updatedInput` field (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput — REQUIRED, else Claude Code ignores the rewrite and the PR is created but never merged) to append ` && gh pr merge --squash --admin --delete-branch` and, IF cwd is a linked git worktree (not the main one), additionally appends ` && (cd <main-wt> && git worktree remove <this-wt> --force 2>/dev/null && git branch -D <branch> 2>/dev/null || true)` — transparent atomic create → merge → worktree+branch cleanup. The worktree+branch are detected at hook-time (`git rev-parse --show-toplevel` · `git worktree list --porcelain` first entry · `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD`), so the cleanup targets the exact context the PR was issued from. Enforces commons @D g47 (PR ship discipline — no unmerged stacks, no dead worktrees). Skip conditions: `--draft` flag present · command already contains `gh pr merge`. NO opt-out by design. Also ships a `/pr-cycle` command (manual one-shot trigger): pushes the current branch (`git push -u origin HEAD`) then `gh pr create --fill` — the hook then auto-appends merge + cleanup, so one command does create → merge → clean. A cross-repo create (`--repo X`) routes the appended merge to that repo (`gh pr merge --repo X`) and skips the local worktree cleanup, since the cwd repo ≠ the PR's repo (0.3.6 · INBOX #4). Refuses on main/master.
